PokeZero checkpoint trait tracking

82 metric sets · 72 self-play · 10 foul-play · lineages: l200-ep7-wu75, l200-seq, m50-ep7, m50-seq, v22-lr3m

Phase 1 — self-play basics over training

Excluded for legibility: v22-lr3m@100k (stalls ~50% of games to the turn cap; its scale compresses the other lineages). The point is retained in the by-checkpoint trajectories below.

m50-ep7l200-ep7-wu75v22-lr3mm50-seql200-seq

Top-5 moves at the frontier milestone

lineagemilestonetop-5 moves (share)
m50-ep71600kHidden Power (0.071), Toxic (0.060), Surf (0.055), Substitute (0.053), Earthquake (0.049)
l200-ep7-wu751300kHidden Power (0.085), Toxic (0.057), Substitute (0.051), Earthquake (0.049), Ice Beam (0.045)
v22-lr3m2300kHidden Power (0.073), Toxic (0.058), Surf (0.052), Earthquake (0.051), Substitute (0.048)
m50-seq1000kHidden Power (0.082), Toxic (0.062), Surf (0.051), Substitute (0.049), Earthquake (0.048)
l200-seq1000kHidden Power (0.081), Toxic (0.063), Substitute (0.051), Earthquake (0.048), Thunderbolt (0.044)

Trait breakdowns by checkpoint — self-play trajectories

72 checkpoints across 5 lineages · x-axis = cumulative games · each point is one checkpoint (no aggregation)

m50-ep7l200-ep7-wu75v22-lr3mm50-seql200-seq

category use / seat-game

conditional breakdowns (%)

switch behavior / seat-game

resource / endgame

Per-game trait ↔ win correlation (vs FoulPlay)

Within each checkpoint's foul-play games: did the bot use the trait more in games it won? One row per game, so n is games rather than checkpoints — this is the powered version of the aggregate chart below. Confound to keep in mind: longer games contain more of everything, and game length is not independent of the result. 10 checkpoints, 9,736 decided seat-games total. Bar = mean r across checkpoints; whisker = min..max. Bold = every checkpoint agrees in sign (consistency across independent checkpoints is the signal, not any single r).

Per-game trait ↔ win correlation (self-play, paired)

The best-controlled view we have. Both seats are the same policy playing the same game, so comparing the winning seat's behavior against the losing seat's holds policy strength and game length fixed by construction — a game-level quantity has no within-game variance and cannot leak in. 72 checkpoints, 314,296 decided seat-games total. Bar = mean r across checkpoints; whisker = min..max. Bold = every checkpoint agrees in sign (consistency across independent checkpoints is the signal, not any single r).

Trait ↔ foul-play win-rate correlation

Pearson r across 10 checkpoints. Both the trait and the win rate are measured on the same foul-play games (m50-ep7@500k 0.20, m50-ep7@1000k 0.35, l200-ep7-wu75@500k 0.32, l200-ep7-wu75@800k 0.30, v22-lr3m@500k 0.31, v22-lr3m@1900k 0.42, m50-seq@500k 0.34, m50-seq@1000k 0.39, l200-seq@500k 0.32, l200-seq@1000k 0.35). Green = trait tracks a higher foul-play win rate, red = lower.

One point per checkpoint, not per game: each checkpoint's 1000 foul-play games collapse into a single (trait, win-rate) pair, so n is the number of checkpoints — game volume buys precision within a point, not more points. It is also an aggregate correlation confounded by overall checkpoint strength (better models do more of everything effective and win more), so read it as association, not cause.

n=10 checkpoints spanning a 0.222 win-rate range.

Phase 2 — detailed panel by checkpoint (self vs foul-play)

Full breakdown for every checkpoint with both self-play and foul-play (m50-ep7@500k, m50-ep7@1000k, l200-ep7-wu75@500k, l200-ep7-wu75@800k, v22-lr3m@500k, v22-lr3m@1900k, m50-seq@500k, m50-seq@1000k, l200-seq@500k, l200-seq@1000k) — 500k plus each lineage's most recent checkpoint. Self-play and foul-play are kept separate, never merged.

opponent = self
